internal_anchors.details: test.js suite passed all 3758 checks including Section 3 Internal Links. No manual re-check required per prompt rules.

tab_structure.details: test.js Phase A Design Refresh checks passed. All tabs and nav-chain verified.

external_links.notes: www.noaa.gov returns HTTP 403 to automated curl but this is a known bot-blocking pattern — the site is up and non-DOI NOAA sub-domain links all resolve correctly. Classified informational-only, not broken.
external_links.details: All checked data-source links (NOAA sub-domains, ESA, NASA JPL, INTERMAGNET, GitHub, funwithscience.net) return 200. www.noaa.gov root returns 403 to curl (bot-blocking, not broken).

data_prose_consistency.details: wins.json has 72 entries (70 dome-claimed + 2 collision sub-claims 057b/058b). HTML correctly uses counts.total=70 everywhere (computed by excluding /^\d+[a-z]$/ IDs). Summary table has 72 <tr> rows matching all wins. Verdict tallies: Refuted-by-Data=12, Self-Contradicted=20, Misleading=16, Std-Model=18, Not-Demonstrated=3, Unfalsifiable=3. All meta tags present (description, og:title, og:description, ld+json ClaimReview). No data-prose mismatches found.

win_detail_consistency.details: All 72 wins have tldr_evidence and tldr_verdict fields. Progressive disclosure: 358 <details> elements, 360 TLDR elements, 0 empty TLDRs. Hardcoded light colors in sections.json: 0. All checks pass.

build_reproducibility.details: Rebuilt HTML differs from committed only at line 346: build date stamp 'May 30, 2026' → 'May 31, 2026'. This is dynamically generated at build time (new Date().toLocaleDateString()) and is expected behavior on a new day. NOT a content drift. The committed HTML was built on May 30 by an earlier agent run.

discoverability.details: docs/llms.txt exists (5628 bytes, references funwithscience.net/dome-model-review, count '72 WIN' matches wins.json length). docs/sitemap.xml valid XML with urlset, contains main page + llms.txt. docs/robots.txt has Allow: / and Sitemap: directive. All meta tags present.

expansion_tracker.details: Two major issues found: (1) next_id=475 but live items include EXP-471 through EXP-478 (live_max=478) — collision imminent on next allocation. ISS-2296 claimed to fix the previous next_id inversion (next_id=448 vs EXP-448 live) but the fix set next_id=475 while live items had already advanced to 478. Needs: next_id = 479. (2) Four orphaned EXP files have no tracker entry (live or archive): EXP-437, EXP-438, EXP-441, EXP-442. These are referenced in multiple open ISS-230x curmudgeon-hole issues but the tracker itself has no record. Total IDs tracked: 368, live: 27, archived: 391, live_max: 478, arch_max: 458.

curmudgeon_digest.details: Digest regenerated cleanly. 639 total review files, 274 unique base IDs (latest cycle), 601 processed, 3 unprocessed. The 3 unprocessed (WIN-023.c4.json, WIN-066.c6.json, EXP-452-P2-P3-P4-P6.c1.json) all have today's timestamp (2026-05-31 01:09) — written in the overnight cycle, within normal decider pipeline lag. No broken review files.

workspace_only_files.details: 1 workspace-only curmudgeon review not in git: WIN-003.c3.json. At risk of silent loss if workspace is recycled. Analyst expansions: all clear.

iss_id_integrity.details: PROP-063 audit: 0 duplicate IDs in closed-issues.json (2160 entries). 0 cross-file collisions (61 open, 2160 closed). Clean.

prop059_mech_a_bypass.details: 4 CRITICAL Mech-A-bypass candidates in closed-issues.json, all from 2026-05-30T14:43-14:47Z decider run, post-PROP-059 cutoff (2026-05-25T15:00Z), no audit_grandfathered field: ISS-2295 (fixed_by: decider-prop059-verification), ISS-2296 (fixed_by: decider-self-verify), ISS-2297 (fixed_by: decider-self-verify), ISS-2298 (fixed_by: decider-dedup-step). All status='fixed' with no verification_pattern. These appear to be a 'self-verify' batch where the decider verified its own integrity-finding closes without recording fingerprints.

fuse_staleness.details: PROP-064 audit: 1 stale prompt file (FUSE vs git hash mismatch): monitor/prompts/reference/analyst-mode5-prospective.md. First occurrence (not seen in prior runs), classified moderate. Fix: run node build.js sync-workspace from clone. tracked_under: PROP-064.

drift_audit.details: Drift audit was 72.1h stale — refreshed. Result: 3 candidates (0 verdict_changed, 0 new, 2 large_rewrite, 1 tldr_only), 72 audited, 62 non-drifting, 26 pre-tldr-schema (legacy, expected). Low candidate count is healthy.

workspace_sync_skips.details: 0 direction violations in last 24h. 199 mtime-guard skips (normal — git versions newer, working as designed). No escalation needed.

issues_found.description: PROP-059 Mech-A-bypass: 4 ISS entries closed by decider on 2026-05-30 without verification_pattern, post PROP-059 cutoff, no audit_grandfathered field. ISS-2295 (fixed_by: decider-prop059-verification), ISS-2296 (fixed_by: decider-self-verify), ISS-2297 (fixed_by: decider-self-verify), ISS-2298 (fixed_by: decider-dedup-step). Decider performed a self-verify cleanup batch on its own integrity finding ISSes without recording verification_pattern fingerprints. Note: ISS-2296 claimed to fix the next_id inversion but set next_id=475 while live items already reached 478 — incomplete fix.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Decider: (1) Re-verify each target — ISS-2295→ISS-2216 template in sections.json Part 2b, ISS-2296→expansion-tracker next_id (now needs 479 not 475), ISS-2297→EXP-436/439/445 archive moves (are they done?), ISS-2298→archive dedup (are duplicates gone?). (2) For each confirmed-fixed entry, add verification_pattern with grep/node command that confirms the fix is present. (3) Add audit_grandfathered field only for entries confirmed correct out-of-band. (4) For ISS-2296's incomplete fix: set next_id=479 in expansion-tracker.json.

issues_found.description: Expansion-tracker next_id collision imminent: next_id=475 but live items already include EXP-471, EXP-472, EXP-473, EXP-474, EXP-475, EXP-476, EXP-477, EXP-478 (live_max=478). The ISS-2296 fix on 2026-05-30 set next_id=475 but failed to account for live items that had already been allocated up to EXP-478. Next allocation will collide with existing EXP-475.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Set next_id = 479 (= live_max 478 + 1). Self-heal: node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const t=JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync('monitor/analyst/expansion-tracker.json','utf8')); t.next_id=479; fs.writeFileSync('monitor/analyst/expansion-tracker.json',JSON.stringify(t,null,2));"

issues_found.description: 4 orphaned EXP output files have no tracker entry (live or archive): EXP-437.json, EXP-438.json, EXP-441.json, EXP-442.json. All 4 exist in monitor/analyst/expansions/ and in git (committed by workspace-sync 2026-05-30T04:10:34Z). They are referenced in open curmudgeon-hole issues (EXP-437 in ISS-2301/2302/2303/2304; EXP-438 in ISS-2310/2311; EXP-441 in ISS-2321/2334/2337; EXP-442 in ISS-2320/2321/2322) but the tracker itself has no record of them. Decider cannot integrate them without a tracker entry.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Add tracker entries for EXP-437, EXP-438, EXP-441, EXP-442 (status=complete, output_file set). Use next_id=479+ for any new allocations after fixing next_id first. Check each EXP file's content to determine category and win_id for the tracker entry.

issues_found.description: Workspace-only curmudgeon review not committed to git: monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/WIN-003.c3.json. File exists in FUSE workspace but not in git (git ls-files returns nothing). At risk of silent data loss if workspace is recycled. Digest can process FUSE-local files but git-backed agents won't see it.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Commit WIN-003.c3.json to git from a fresh clone: cp from workspace to clone, git add + git commit + git push.

issues_found.description: PROP-064 audit: monitor/prompts/reference/analyst-mode5-prospective.md differs between FUSE and git (md5 mismatch). First occurrence on this run. The analyst-baby agent reads prompts from FUSE for orientation; if this file is stale, baby may execute against an outdated mode-5 procedure.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Run `node build.js sync-workspace` from a fresh clone to sync git-owned prompt files to FUSE. If staleness persists across two integrity runs, file a tinker directive (PROP-061/064 path may have regressed).

summary: Overall status: FAIL. Test suite: 3758/3758 passed. Build reproducibility: pass (only diff is dynamic build-date stamp, expected). Two pressing structural issues: (1) expansion-tracker next_id=475 is ALREADY BELOW live_max=478 — collision imminent, needs immediate fix to 479. This is a regression from the ISS-2296 fix yesterday which set 475 while live items had already reached 478. (2) PROP-059 Mech-A-bypass: 4 ISS closures from yesterday's decider run (ISS-2295/2296/2297/2298) used decider-self-verify / decider-prop059-verification mechanisms without recording verification_pattern fingerprints — exactly the hallucination pattern PROP-059 was designed to catch. Also: 4 orphaned EXP files (437/438/441/442) exist in git but have no tracker entries; 1 workspace-only curmudgeon review (WIN-003.c3.json) is at risk of loss; 1 FUSE-stale prompt file (analyst-mode5-prospective.md, tracked under PROP-064, first occurrence). No broken internal anchors, no broken data-source links, no ISS ID collisions, no direction-violation sync skips. Drift audit refreshed: 3 low-priority candidates (2 large_rewrite, 1 tldr_only). 3 unprocessed curmudgeon reviews (WIN-023.c4, WIN-066.c6, EXP-452-P2-P3-P4-P6.c1) are fresh from tonight — normal pipeline lag.
